title: Control Architecture and experimental validation of a Novel Surgical Robotic Instrument
abstract: 微创手术（MIS）能减轻患者创伤并缩短恢复时间，但传统腹腔镜器械仍受限于活动范围不足。本研究提出一种四自由度柔性腹腔镜器械的控制架构，该器械集成了末端弯曲、独立末端头旋转、轴体旋转及夹持功能，同时保持10毫米直径以兼容标准套管针。驱动单元与SpaceMouse遥操作通过Raspberry Pi 5平台结合Motoron控制器实现。研究推导并参数化了一种解析式剪刀连杆模型，其预测的钳口开合角度与CAD测量结果（平均绝对误差0.13°）及OptiTrack运动捕捉数据（平均绝对误差1.43°）相符。通过与ATHENA并联机器人集成，在模拟胰腺手术流程中验证了系统的有效性。
preview_text: Minimally invasive surgery (MIS) reduces patient trauma and shortens recovery time; however, conventional laparoscopic instruments remain constrained by limited range of movements. This work presents the control architecture of a 4-DOF flexible laparoscopic instrument integrating distal bending, independent distal head rotation, shaft rotation, and a gripper, while maintaining a 10 mm diameter compatible with standard trocars. The actuation unit and SpaceMouse teleoperation are implemented on Raspberry Pi 5 with Motoron controllers. An analytical scissor-linkage model is derived and parameterized. The predicted jaw opening corresponds to CAD measurements (MAE 0.13{\textdegree}) and OptiTrack motion capture (MAE 1.43{\textdegree}). Integration with the ATHENA parallel robot is validated through a simulated pancreatic surgery procedure.
one_line_summary: 这篇论文介绍了一种新型手术机器人仪器的控制架构和实验验证，专注于机械设计、建模和集成，与强化学习、VLA、运动控制等关键词无关。
